title: [lichen_l2.c:1237 vs lora_l2.c:421-432] init race: lora_l2_init idempotency allows partial state
description: Cross-file analysis: lichen_l2_iface_init() calls lichen_lora_l2_init() at line 1237. lora_l2.c:init() is idempotent (lines 421-423, 429-432), returning 0 if already initialized. However, if a PREVIOUS partial init left the state machine in STOPPED (init succeeded but start failed later), a new lichen_l2_iface_init() would see init() return 0, but lora_data.eui64 is valid from the first init while lora_data.rx_callback may be stale. This is actually handled correctly because init() only transitions UNINIT->STOPPED, and STOPPED means the first init completed successfully. The concern is if init() could leave state UNINIT with partial lora_data populated - examining the code shows it sets lora_data fields before transition, so failure after setting fields but before transition would leave state UNINIT with partial data. However, the fields are re-set on next init attempt, so this is safe. Downgrade to documentation: the idempotency guarantee should note that partial state is reset on retry.
close_reason: Fixed: Added idempotency guarantee documentation in lora_l2.h lichen_lora_l2_init() explaining that partial state from failed attempts is always overwritten on retry.
